items.title: Mumumelon: the campaign using dupes as a “vehicle” for climate activism
items.description: Climate activist group Action Speaks Louder argues Mumumelon campaign does not “directly copy” Lululemon clothing.

items.title: Win without gain: Microsoft prevails in cost dispute with Suinno
items.description: Finnish company Suinno is the owner of EP 2 671 173 covering a “method and means for browsing by walking”. It relates to people browsing the internet by walking to discover search results as they come into close proximity. The Helsinki-based company accuses Microsoft of...

items.title: Friday Fantasies
items.description: While many have enjoyed a shorter work week, the IPKat has been busy keeping an eye out for events and opportunities that might interest our dear readers. Here's the latest updates.Forthcoming EventsImage by Gundula Vogel via PixabayThe next edition of the People, Plants and the...

items.title: LEGO Minifigure win; Yuga Labs settlement; Ozempic copycat ruling – news digest
items.description: Six bite-sized IP updates from the week, also featuring Apollonia's settlement with the Prince estate, Jo Malone’s response to her name dispute with Estée Lauder, and Amazon's Counterfeit Crimes Unit launch in India.

items.title: Trump signals brand strategy shift with first TTAB cancellation actions
items.description: The principal trademark holding company for President Donald Trump is seeking cancellation of third-party registrations before the TTAB for what appears to be the first time.

items.title: New patent owner Silimed lands PI against Polytech at Munich court
items.description: Silimed has achieved its first success in the infringement dispute over breast implant technology. After the Brazilian manufacturer secured the rights to a patent for the manufacture of breast implants in a complex entitlement action, Silimed is now taking measures against its...

items.title: Building “scalable” IP frameworks for the QVC Group
items.description: Generative AI has created both opportunities and obstacles for the live social shopping industry. The QVC Group’s assistant general counsel for IP is working on proactive, scalable solutions, she tells WTR.

items.title: Amendments to India’s Designs Act “more urgent” than ever
items.description: The Calcutta High Court recently held that graphical user interfaces qualify as registrable designs, but several questions remain unanswered.

items.title: [GuestPost] Keeping enemies closer: C&J Clark v TBC and trade mark coexistence agreements
items.description: The IPKat is grateful to Katfriend Thomas Hood (Gatehouse Chambers) for the following contribution on the recent UK decision in C & J Clark International ltd v Trek Bicycle Corporation & Anor [2026] EWHC 659 (Ch). Over to Tom:In the world of trade marks, businesses can often end...

items.title: US Supreme Court vacates $47 million verdict against internet service provider after consequential Cox ruling
items.description: The impact of the US Supreme Court’s recent ruling in Cox is already being felt, as a major copyright liability verdict against another internet service provider has been sent back to the lower court.

items.title: Dyson wins another PI in hair-styler fight against Dreame
items.description: Once again, the Hamburg local division under presiding judge Sabine Klepsch has prohibited Chinese manufacturer Dreame from distributing certain hair styling devices in the UPC territory and Spain by means of a preliminary injunction. A PI issued in August last year covered...

items.title: UAE and Bahrain grant temporary filing relief amid Middle East conflict – IP office updates
items.description: Our latest round-up also covers the Korean Ministry of Intellectual Property publishing its updated Goods Guidebook and Nepal clarifying the standards for trademark rejection.

items.title: Lost no more: Australia finally unlocks the door for orphan works
items.description: Australia’s first-ever orphan works scheme has officially commenced after the Copyright Amendment Bill received Royal Assent on 1 April 2026.

items.title: How the Federation of the Swiss Watch Industry is strengthening its fight against counterfeiting
items.description: The Federation of the Swiss Watch Industry explains its strategy for tackling counterfeits across key jurisdictions and disposing of illicit goods sustainably.

items.title: Never Too Late: If you missed the IPKat last week!
items.description: Combing through legal archives and chocolate eggs is learned, but exhausting. If you, much like the Kats, spent the past few days taking a well-deserved Easter nap to sleep off the chocolate, you will be pleased to know it was a relatively quiet week on the blog too. Here is a...

items.title: Trust is not enough: German Supreme Court tightens liability for Google Ads
items.description: In a landmark ruling in Google-Ads (I ZR 28/25), the German Supreme Court dealt with the question whether a retailer is liable for unlawful advertisements placed by Google on third-party platforms. Background The claimant is a registered qualified trade association whose members...

items.title: China introduces new guidelines for cross-border e-commerce in response to EU crackdown on small parcels
items.description: China has introduced new guidelines aimed at raising standards in cross-border e-commerce, as European authorities move to tighten controls on small parcels entering the EU.

items.title: Lundbeck and Biogaran fight in Milan and Paris over drug for alcohol dependence
items.description: Nalmefene is an opioid antagonist used in the EU to reduce alcohol dependence. The active ingredient works by antagonising those receptors in the brain responsible for producing the body’s feel-good chemical, dopamine. This prevents the alcohol-induced release of this chemical,...

items.title: [Book Review] Stone on European Union Design Law: A Practitioners’ Guide
items.description: This is a review of the third edition of Stone on European Union Design Law: A Practitioners’ Guide (Oxford University Press, 2025), authored by David Stone. More than twenty years after the European Community Design system came into force, one might have expected the dust to...

items.title: [Guest post] The TRIPS non-violation moratorium has expired: What happened in Yaoundé, and what comes next
items.description: The IPKat is happy to host the guest post below by Daniel J. Gervais (Vanderbilt University), detailing and reflecting on the recently concluded 14th Ministerial Conference of the World Trade Organization (WTO). Here’s what he writes:The TRIPS non-violation moratorium has...

items.title: [Guest post] No cultural funding from a collecting society at its own discretion and not for unauthorised parties
items.description: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest contribution by Norbert P. Flechsig (Faculty of Law, University of Tübingen, Germany) concerning the recent Opinion of Advocate General (AG) Szpunar in VG WORT, C-840/24. Here’s what he writes: No cultural funding...

items.title: HGF boosts London chemistry and pharma practice with D Young partner
items.description: Garreth Duncan officially joined HGF’s London office on 1 April 2026, bringing with him considerable expertise across a broad range of chemical technologies. His practice has a strong focus on the pharmaceutical sector, where he specialises in protecting new chemical entities,...

items.title: Next US firm enters London patent market after CMS hire
items.description: It is not yet decided when Caitlin Heard will move to Perkins Coie as partner. Managing IP first reported the move. Heard currently serves as co-head of the international IP practice group at CMS Cameron McKenna Nabarro Olswang. She is qualified as a solicitor advocate and also...

items.title: De Koffiejongens averts PI filed by Ox Barrier in the Netherlands
items.description: The case is reminiscent of the patent dispute over coffee capsules between manufacturers Nespresso, Krups, De’Longhi and Ethical Coffee Company (ECC), which broke out between 2016 and 2018 in Germany and Switzerland, as well as the more recent dispute between Koninklijke Douwe...

items.title: Klaus Grabinski: “Economic logic suggests the UPC will continue to grow”
items.description: JUVE Patent: Mr Grabinski, you have now been President of the Unified Patent Court for over three years – initially during the preparatory phase and, since 1 June 2023, of the operational court. How satisfied are you with the current developement? Klaus Grabinski: I’m very...

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds PI against Sinocare and Menarini
items.description: The local division The Hague has become attractive for PI applications — especially in the medical devices sector. In Abbott’s dispute with Chinese competitor Sinocare and the latter’s distribution partner Menarini, the court thus appears to have delivered what companies and...

items.title: Chinese car makers take Avanci licence for connected cars
items.description: Avanci today announced the conclusion of ten licence agreements to date across its 4G Vehicle and 5G Vehicle licensing programmes. While not naming the seven car manufacturers in its press release, the patent pool hinted at further licensing deals to come in the months ahead....
